payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Her eyes close for a minute and she nods. "The coming fight does indeed warrant the aid that these journeys would muster and I can see the touch of my brethren on you already." And she motions towards Vax and Vex and Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What do you wish to know?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well. Do you intend to return to face this entity, or have you chosen or found champions in Exandria who would rise up against this individual?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We didn't even realize that was an option.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hadn't occurred to us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wow. Maybe that's very self-centered of us, now that I think about it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We intend to face him down ourselves.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We want to fight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're heading his direction no matter what.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Do you think you have the strength required to do this?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're gathering the strength right now. Some among us have already-- the one who's making weird faces has already sworn allegiance to another god.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hashtag blessed.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Then let the challenge be placed before you. Scanlan Shorthalt! Somewhere within this library lies the book that contains the Incantation of Isolation, the arcane rite that banished the Chained Oblivion. You have one hour to retrieve it. Your friends can help you, but you can only present one tome to me. And should it be incorrect, then I shall await another warrior of keen mind to find me and accept my gift, for I can only guide one strong enough to face the foe before you. Return to me when you are certain you've found it. Your challenge begins now." And with that, her entire body vanishes in a gust of wind. All the little tendrils of paper flutter out and come to rest within the dome, and you look up and see the immensity of this library.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not to question your infinite knowledge and judgment, but my stories are more of little bar room tales and silly poems to pass the time. I would hardly call them epic histories of civilization.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "For now, perhaps."

preceding_turns.speaker: DARIN
preceding_turns.text: We're all stories in the end. Just make sure yours is a good one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Do you think that your compatriots would have the strength now to rise against such a scourge had you not been there to lift their spirits with these tales?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's true, Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm really powerful.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And humble.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's my best asset.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And deeper than you let on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And the cube.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) And a cube.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She glances over at you with a knowing look.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: If you have faith in me, I will have faith in you and trust you and your knowledge. And I will try to complete any task you ask of me.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. You close your eyes and focus and you begin to beseech the knowledge and wisdom of the Dawnfather. Two minutes pass with no answer.

preceding_turns.speaker: DARIN
preceding_turns.text: Not anymore. Burny-burny, ashy-ashy. Bye-bye.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you remember the name of it?

preceding_turns.speaker: DARIN
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try casting Locate Object and cast it on the tome that contains the Incantation of Isolation. Try it. Why not.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can it work like that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: An object known to you as long as you have seen it up close within 30 feet at least once.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. It's a spell I have now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Describe or name an object that is familiar to you. You sense the direction of the object's location, so as long as the object is within a thousand feet of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you cast the spell. And you think about it and you concentrate. Nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to kneel down and pray to the Dawnfather, since he was one of the ones that helped fight the Chained Oblivion, to see if he could guide us at all.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's been about 20 minutes, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's always good to lie.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well she tasked me; it's not a lie! She tasked me with this. We seek a book. We would love some help, and it contains the Incantation of Isolation. Maybe about 812 years old-ish. Maybe 813?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No one seems to be paying attention to your words.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe sing it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I have to sing it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pull out Mythcarver.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pull out Mythcarver and sing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. I pull out a sword. (sword unsheathing)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just sing like it's a microphone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. (singing) Just take a look, it's in a book, the Scanlan rainbow. I can read anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as the same librarians all of a sudden stop what they're doing and turn back towards you. The sword is vibrating and ringing; it's expanding the notes of your song, as is the nature of Mythcarver. You seem to have their attention.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Now ask. Now sing and question.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 22. Okay. They continue to push at a brisk pace and you watch as other of the spectral librarians begin to gather into this group and is now a cluster of about 25 of them that are snaking around these bookcases and traveling at a pretty fair pace. Which once was walking pace, and then went to a jogging pace, they're now darting through. And they're leading you quickly away from the center of the library where everyone else seems to be clustered. So the two of you are following and swooshing behind them, you're going over and around bookcases. Some of the bookcases you see have giant holes in the center and you can dart through and dive past. The speed is increasing to the point where the wind is starting to kick up in your hair and you're amazed at the speed of these librarians and the fact that they're being driven by your very song, and also the fact that you have no idea how far away the rest of your friends are in this library, and this library seems to just go on and on and on. You continue forward for another ten minutes or so, so you've hit just over the 30-minute mark at this point, and you feel this little pulse from the stone. Do you continue to inspire?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Keep telling the story.

preceding_turns.speaker: DARIN
preceding_turns.text: Tell them the song about Vecna just might wreck ya. It's close; it doesn't work.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, Sprigg, I got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear Sprigg yelling from down below, as he doesn't have an earring.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) If we don't act soon Vecna might just wreck ya. (speaking) I got it in there for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: DARIN
preceding_turns.text: Thanks. Tell them about your neck, ma.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah. Wait, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: DARIN
preceding_turns.text: If you're discussing your neck to your mother, you would-- what? Oh, right. People get paid for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whispers) Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: DARIN
preceding_turns.text: Call back to earlier discussion. And nobody knows.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) God I hope I get it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) The book is eight centuries old, or so I'm told. It's fraught with mystery. It's teeming with history. So please listen to me and don't kiss me because I really need this book right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll another performance check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Come on.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You have about 15 minutes left and it's about ten minutes of travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I could potentially grab all of them and shove them in the Bag of Colding.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And then what? How many minutes are left, and how far are we from our friends?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes you about a minute to throw them all in there haphazardly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You pull out the one with nothing on it. It's a very thin book; it's only about that wide or so, it's a dark gray leather. It looks damaged. Part of it's burnt and scarred on the outside. There's no words on the cover. You open it up and it's about four pages that are blank.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is the thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What can you do? Do you have a spell that lets you see things? Reveals things?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I do not.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Talk to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Sing to it or something!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure. I'll sing to a book, Matt. (singing) Read to me of times gone by. Can it be you're here when a bunch of gods died? (speaking) Okay. That's all I got.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't seem to have any effect. Doesn't seem to respond to the singing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't tell anyone I sang to a book.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You just keep singing, I'll keep taking books. We've got not enough time.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Very well." She takes it and looks at it. "It has been a while since I thumbed through these pages myself. This will do you well in the coming fray." And she hands it out to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I casually blow the hair out of my face and walk over to her and say, O mighty goddess, it was a challenging challenge, but I think you'll be pretty happy with the results. As I hold up the book, I'll cast Wrist Pocket and make it disappear. Oh god, oh it's gone! Just kidding. And it's back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She doesn't flinch.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is what we've found for you. I hope it pleases you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And this is your final choice?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You know, we didn't actually look at the other ten books.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Regis Philbin.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that your final answer?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes. With conviction, I firmly believe that this is the correct book.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The smug clapping comes to a halt. He nods. "You're the only ones to get away. Others found me before you did, and another group of supposed heroes came and assaulted me shortly after you vanished. No matter. They all failed. And rose again."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) "Welcome back. You must have traveled quite a distance to be far from my sight."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is he see-through? I try to shoot an arrow at him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, roll for an attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Cocked.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, that's not cocked. Cocked! 29.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 29. (whoosh) It passes through his body.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I fucking knew it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He laughs for a second, and you see him smiling his terrible grin across curled, yellowed teeth. You see that flicker of green energy sparking in his left eye. It seems to pulse as his brow arches above it. "I admire your resolve. You did it. You solved the riddle. You found my home, ruined my tower, slew one of my greatest acolytes, and even then, you failed. Still, impressive. That is most definitely worthy of a fine round of applause."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I am Vecna the Unholy. I am god upon Exandria. I come to play with the toys left by insolent, forgotten ancients and make this world into a new epoch. What this means, however, is an end to all. An end to borders and nations. There will only be the will of Vecna. So. Ask yourselves. You want peace? There is no peace, the way things are. You all bicker and stab and murder in the names of your mothers, your fathers. But this one? Everyone will live, and when they die, that need not be the end, either, under my banner. I can see your every move. I know your plot. I know your destination. I know your plan, your capabilities, your allies, and your imminent demise, should you continue. I offer you the chance to stand down, discard your imprisoned gods, and pledge to me. Many you love will. Many you love already have."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Look at all these big-ass trees.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Also, is there anybody in the forest?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Natural 19.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's plenty to see, and you don't see anybody else nearby in the forest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Path of least resistance without traps?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you see a path you can take.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not entirely certain I am mortal anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I know what you are, and I know you know where I am. Come to me whenever you like."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, fuckstick, we are coming.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Are you all too bloody daft to see? It's over. I'm no longer the Undying King. I am the King of all Kings. I am not the Scourge in Secret. I am the Tyrant Unmatched."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you're very grand. And I begin to fingerfuck his eyehole in the illusion. So majestic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As soon as your finger goes forward, his image flickers out and appears in the same room, behind you, now right up against Percy and Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We need to go.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the
payload.raw_text: Okay, you're heading to the Raven's Crest. All right. You make your way to the--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: We should probably try and use our words as much as possible.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Blue and a foot smaller, like Blue Man Group. Black clothes, blue face, bald.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: So you have a little more of an aasimar appearance, though a larger one than you've seen. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right, where are you guys charging off to?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 21? All right. Keeping an eye out, you don't see anybody following you at the moment. That does not appear to be an issue. So you head to the area of the Duskmeadow, to the southeast of the city. You come through the dry, cold, and dusty sector. A little bit of snow appears to have melted into slush at the corners, but most of it has been pushed off the main roads to the side. You see dirty snow matted at the edges. You follow Vax very quickly through the streets, and you see a few of the veiled maidens of the Raven's Crest aerie taking note of you. And as you approach, they just point over to the Raven's Crest silently as you walk past.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fuck! All right, I'll cast Seeming on you. On all of us. Because I can.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Will you make me look like Tary?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, what? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's not smart!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, it's not like he was in the room where it happened.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The room where it happened?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The room where it happened.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Keep running toward the Raven Queen temple as that's happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We should probably try and use our words as much as possible.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Blue and a foot smaller, like Blue Man Group. Black clothes, blue face, bald.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you have a little more of an aasimar appearance, though a larger one than you've seen. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, where are you guys charging off to?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Raven Queen temple.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you're heading to the Raven's Crest. All right. You make your way to the--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to keep looking around, make sure no one's tailing us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make a perception check.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys, after waiting for about 30 minutes, it feels like, of tense silence, you watch as the surface of the pool (splashing) splashes upwards as a crimson Vax comes to the surface. Not gasping for breath. Just stoic, as he comes to the edge of the pool, step after step, dripping the blood with each footprint on the ground. Your fist hurts a bit from the tightness, but as you unfurl your fingers you look down and see a small, onyx opal. It's a stone about the size of a gem that big on your hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's a long, silent stare. "I put my faith in you, my fate-touched. Do not forget that. And all I ask is you put all your faith in me."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You have it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Then I shall watch your thread. Now wake up." In that instant, everything goes black once more, and you feel the pain, the heat in your lungs, but then it subsides quickly, and you stay there beneath the familiar, thick surface of the blood. No need for air.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're just chilling like a villain under the blood?!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) He can breathe blood!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The instinct to breathe is still there, but you realize after a moment of panic that it's not necessary.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I have what I came for?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can't see. Your body is clenched tight. Your muscles are still tense.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'll move up.

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to the Braving
payload.raw_text: Okay. The three of you make your way to the Braving Grounds.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You create the shimmering doorway, and you guys come into the mansion without issue.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'll don the hat so I turn into the Meat Man so that no one can recognize me.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the Trial
payload.raw_text: You make your way to the Trial Forge. The doors are currently closed, it is mid-evening at this point. It's probably, at this point, with the communion and you guys making your way across the city? Yeah, it's close to midnight.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: We'll ask Keyleth when we get back.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. We sent it to the plane of the Dread Emperor.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: She knows how to get there.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You guys make your way through the Proving Grounds. Where are you heading to specifically?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: To the temple of Kord. Big man outside. The Trial Forge with the big doors.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You make your way to the Trial Forge. The doors are currently closed, it is mid-evening at this point. It's probably, at this point, with the communion and you guys making your way across the city? Yeah, it's close to midnight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No one's seen the Sword of Kas since Vecna was destroyed along with Kas.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Exactly! Craven Edge could be the Sword of Kas.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It got lost and was refound.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: By the Briarwoods. They were going to awaken him, but then he died.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Or it's just a sword.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Or it's just a sword that just likes blood.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We've got to do something.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can we go?!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, Grog. Yes, of course. We're walking. We're doing this while we walk. We're not wasting time with this conversation. We're traveling with this conversation.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I didn't realize.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll ask Keyleth when we get back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. We sent it to the plane of the Dread Emperor.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She knows how to get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys make your way through the Proving Grounds. Where are you heading to specifically?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: To the temple of Kord. Big man outside. The Trial Forge with the big doors.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, he's been back to you and looking over the shoulder. He finally turns and gives a nudge to one of the monks up top, who darts off. (running noises) A few awkward quiet minutes pass. (running noises) Jumps down into the sand pit, runs up, and hands you a small satchel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is that near the Fields of Elysium, or a totally different place?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I do not know."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Listen, he is very busy right now with his meditation garden.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right, yeah. So you have nothing else that you can do? I know that you're going to fight his troops, but the fact of the matter is Vecna is a god. If we can't find some way to speak to Kord, we may not be able to save the world again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Action now would prevent the spread of his power.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If what you say is true, I'm certain Kord already knows. We will do what we can, but what you ask is not within our ability to grant."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fair enough. You don't happen to have a bean on you, do you? Hard no?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Powerful beans. Super.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For the first time, you see Kord glance over his shoulder at you--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) He hasn't looked at us this whole fucking time?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah. Okay. As you exit the temple, it's late night, and the bustle has calmed down a little bit, as it is the evening, but you do see there are-- normally what are small patrol lights at the edges of the walls, you can see large fires are burning at the edges to extend eyesight. It's interesting. You see wyverns in the sky. You hear the familiar cries of wyverns, and at first, it catches you off-guard. But Vex'ahlia, glancing up, you can see they all have some sort of barding on them. Silver and blue barding.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. He's in Ysgard.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're saying it weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: "He's-god."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: "He's-god." So should I change back?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I can't do it again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean? You can't?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I guess I could do it one more time, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're going back to sleep, right? It's either that or I put a fancy robe over my whole face.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This time, I make us all blue.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lovely. Can I have light blue hair?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Dark blue skin, light blue hair? Sure. I'll be opposite.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not painted bald caps? Not blue-painted bald caps?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, I want beautiful hair. I've always wanted blue hair.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's not the Blue Man Group I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We make our way back to the temples. Is the bustle the same outside of the temple?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, as you step out of the temple and Scanlan blues you all, you make your way across--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Episode title  The Blue Job. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The multiple blue jobs.
